name: ClientDataManager
description: Main data management class that wraps react-query's QueryClient. Provides tag-based cache invalidation, source-level invalidation, and reset methods. Used as the central data layer for Gravity UI applications.
when_to_use: Setting up the application-wide data management layer
Invalidating cached queries by tag after a mutation
Resetting or clearing specific data sources
avoid: Direct react-query QueryClient usage when data-source patterns are in use — use ClientDataManager methods instead

parameters.name: config.defaultOptions
parameters.description: Default react-query options for all queries (staleTime, retry, etc).

examples: const dataManager = new ClientDataManager({ defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: 300000, retry: 3 } } });

name: composeFullKey
description: Composes a full react-query query key array for a data source, including the source name, its tags, and the composed key string. Used internally by the library to build structured cache keys.
when_to_use: Building a full cache key that includes tags for targeted cache invalidation
Passing to react-query options directly when composing custom query options
avoid: Using as a user-facing key string — prefer composeKey for readable string keys
signature: composeFullKey<TDataSource extends AnyDataSource>(dataSource: TDataSource, params: DataSourceParams<TDataSource>): ReadonlyArray<string>

parameters.name: dataSource
parameters.description: Data source instance created with makePlainQueryDataSource or makeInfiniteQueryDataSource.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Parameters for the query; can be idle.

name: composeInfiniteQueryOptions
description: Composes react-query infinite query observer options from a data source, context, params, and optional overrides. Used internally by useQueryData but available for custom integration with useInfiniteQuery.
when_to_use: When integrating an infinite data source directly with react-query's useInfiniteQuery without useQueryData
avoid: In most cases useQueryData handles this — use composeInfiniteQueryOptions only for advanced custom integration
signature: composeInfiniteQueryOptions(context: DataSourceContext, dataSource: AnyInfiniteQueryDataSource, params: DataSourceParams, options?: Partial<DataSourceOptions>): InfiniteQueryObserverExtendedOptions

name: composeKey
description: Composes a stable string cache key for a data source and its params. Returns a human-readable string like 'user(hash)' or 'user:idle' when params is idle.
when_to_use: Building a string key for cache lookups or logging
Checking whether a query key corresponds to a specific data source
avoid: Using as the full react-query queryKey — use composeFullKey instead for tag support
signature: composeKey<TDataSource extends AnyDataSource>(dataSource: TDataSource, params: DataSourceParams<TDataSource>): string

name: composePlainQueryOptions
description: Composes react-query query observer options from a plain data source, context, params, and optional overrides. Used internally by useQueryData but available for custom integration with useQuery.
when_to_use: When integrating a plain data source directly with react-query's useQuery without useQueryData
avoid: In most cases useQueryData handles this — use composePlainQueryOptions only for advanced custom integration
signature: composePlainQueryOptions(context: DataSourceContext, dataSource: AnyPlainQueryDataSource, params: DataSourceParams, options?: Partial<DataSourceOptions>): QueryObserverExtendedOptions

name: DataInfiniteLoader
description: React component that handles loading, error, and pagination states for infinite queries. Renders LoadingView during initial load, ErrorView on failure, and MoreView for pagination controls.
when_to_use: Displaying paginated or infinite-scroll data with loading and error handling
Wrapping content that depends on an infinite query data source
avoid: Non-paginated data — use DataLoader instead

props.name: error
props.description: Error object from the query, or null.

props.name: hasNextPage
props.description: Whether more pages are available to load.

props.name: fetchNextPage
props.description: Function to trigger loading the next page.

props.name: isFetchingNextPage
props.description: Whether the next page is currently being fetched.

props.name: MoreView
props.description: Component shown for the 'load more' control.

props.name: children
props.description: Content to render when data is available.

examples:
<DataInfiniteLoader
  status={status}
  error={error}
  hasNextPage={hasNextPage}
  fetchNextPage={fetchNextPage}
  isFetchingNextPage={isFetchingNextPage}
  LoadingView={Spinner}
  ErrorView={ErrorMessage}
  MoreView={LoadMoreButton}
>
  {data.map((item) => <Item key={item.id} data={item} />)}
</DataInfiniteLoader>

name: DataLoader
description: React component that handles loading and error states for plain queries. Shows LoadingView while fetching and ErrorView on failure, otherwise renders children.
when_to_use: Wrapping content that depends on a data source query
Displaying a spinner during data fetch and an error message on failure
avoid: Paginated or infinite-scroll data — use DataInfiniteLoader instead

props.name: children
props.description: Content to render when data is loaded.

examples:
<DataLoader status={status} error={error} errorAction={refetch} LoadingView={Spinner} ErrorView={ErrorMessage}>
  {data && <UserCard user={data} />}
</DataLoader>

name: DataManagerProvider
description: React context provider that makes a DataManager instance available to all child components via the DataManagerContext.
when_to_use: Wrapping the application root to provide DataManager to all descendant components
avoid: Providing multiple DataManagers in nested trees — use a single root provider
Use DataSourceProvider instead when using ClientDataManager, as it also sets up QueryClientProvider

name: DataSourceProvider
description: All-in-one React provider that wraps both QueryClientProvider and DataManagerProvider for a ClientDataManager. Also sets up query normalizer subscription if one is configured. Preferred over DataManagerProvider for most applications.
when_to_use: As the single top-level provider when using ClientDataManager
When a query normalizer is configured on the ClientDataManager
avoid: Nesting inside an existing QueryClientProvider — DataSourceProvider creates its own

name: getError
description: Returns the first non-null error from an array of query states, or null if all are error-free. Useful for aggregating errors from multiple queries.
when_to_use: Getting the first error from multiple combined query results
signature: getError<T>(states: { error: T | null }[]): NonNullable<T> | null

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: Array of objects with an error field.

name: getProgressiveRefetch
description: Creates a refetch interval function that progressively increases the interval between refetches up to a maximum, using exponential backoff. Returns a function compatible with react-query's refetchInterval option.
when_to_use: Polling an endpoint with increasing delays to reduce server load over time
Implementing progressive retry logic for long-running background updates
avoid: Static polling — use a plain number for refetchInterval instead

parameters.name: options.minInterval
parameters.description: Minimum refetch interval in milliseconds (used for the first refetch).

parameters.name: options.multiplier
parameters.description: Exponential multiplier for each successive interval. Defaults to 2.

name: getStatus
description: Returns the merged DataLoaderStatus from an array of query states. Errors take priority over loading, which takes priority over success.
when_to_use: Computing a single status from multiple combined query results without creating a full useQueryResponses hook

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: Array of objects with a status field.

name: hasTag
description: Checks whether a query key array contains a specific data source tag. Ignores the first element (source name) and last element (full key string) to match only intermediate tag positions.
when_to_use: Filtering queries during cache invalidation by checking if their key contains a specific tag

name: idle
description: Special symbol that disables query execution when passed as params to useQueryData. The query remains in 'success' status with undefined data, maintaining type safety for conditional queries.
when_to_use: Conditionally skipping a query when required parameters are not yet available
Replacing enabled: false for type-safe conditional queries
avoid: When you need the query to actively cancel — idle prevents execution but doesn't cancel in-flight requests
examples: const { data } = useQueryData(userDataSource, userId ? { userId } : idle);
// Query won't fire until condition is met, status stays 'success'

name: isAbortable
description: Type guard that checks whether a value has an AbortSignal-compatible signal property, indicating it can be used with AbortController cancellation.
when_to_use: Checking if a fetch context or request object supports AbortSignal before wiring cancellation
signature: isAbortable(value: unknown): value is { signal: AbortSignal }

name: isCancellable
description: Type guard that checks whether a value has a cancel() method, indicating it supports the Cancellable interface used by data-source for request cancellation.
when_to_use: Checking if a fetch result object supports cancel() before wiring AbortSignal cancellation

name: makeInfiniteQueryDataSource
description: Creates an infinite query data source definition for pagination and infinite scroll patterns. Wraps react-query's infinite query with data-source conventions including tag-based cache invalidation.
when_to_use: Creating a data source for paginated list APIs
Implementing infinite scroll with automatic next-page detection
avoid: Simple single-fetch data — use makePlainQueryDataSource instead

parameters.name: config.name
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the data source.

parameters.name: config.fetch
parameters.description: Fetch function; use skipContext() to adapt existing API functions.

parameters.name: config.next
parameters.description: Returns params for the next page, or undefined to stop.

parameters.name: config.prev
parameters.description: Returns params for the previous page.

name: makePlainQueryDataSource
description: Creates a plain (single-fetch) query data source definition. Supports parameter and response transformation, tag-based cache invalidation, and react-query options.
when_to_use: Defining a typed data source for a single API endpoint
Adding tag-based cache invalidation to a data fetch
avoid: Paginated or infinite-scroll data — use makeInfiniteQueryDataSource instead

parameters.name: config.name
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the data source.

parameters.name: config.fetch
parameters.description: Fetch function; use skipContext() to adapt existing API functions.

parameters.name: config.transformParams
parameters.description: Transform input params before the request.

examples: const { data, status, error, refetch } = useQueryData(userDataSource, { userId: 123 });

name: mergeStatuses
description: Merges an array of DataLoaderStatus values into a single status. 'error' takes priority, then 'loading', then 'success'.
when_to_use: Combining loading/error states from multiple queries into a single DataLoader-compatible status

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Array of status values to merge.

name: normalizeStatus
description: Converts react-query's QueryStatus and FetchStatus pair into a single DataLoaderStatus value ('loading' | 'success' | 'error'). Handles the pending+fetching combination that maps to 'loading'.
when_to_use: Converting raw react-query status values to data-source's DataLoaderStatus for use with DataLoader
avoid: When using useQueryData — normalization is handled automatically

name: skipContext
description: Utility function that wraps an existing fetch function to skip the context and fetchContext arguments automatically prepended by data-source. Allows using plain API functions without modification.
when_to_use: Adapting existing API functions to work with data-source's fetch interface
avoid: When the function already handles context — implement the full fetch signature instead
signature: skipContext<T extends (...args: any[]) => any>(fn: T): Function

name: useDataManager
description: React hook that returns the DataManager instance from context. Used to imperatively invalidate caches or reset data sources.
when_to_use: Imperatively invalidating queries after mutations
Accessing the DataManager outside of data source hooks
avoid: Reading query data — use useQueryData instead

name: useQueryContext
description: React hook that returns the QueryDataSourceContext, which wraps react-query's QueryClient. Used to pass context to composeInfiniteQueryOptions and composePlainQueryOptions for direct react-query integration.
when_to_use: When manually composing query options with composePlainQueryOptions or composeInfiniteQueryOptions
avoid: In normal component data fetching — use useQueryData instead

name: useQueryData
description: Main React hook for fetching data via a plain or infinite query data source. Returns normalized status, data, error, and refetch alongside the full react-query response.
when_to_use: Fetching data from an API in a React component using a data source
Conditional queries using the idle symbol
avoid: Direct react-query useQuery when data-source patterns are in use
signature: useQueryData(dataSource: AnyDataSource, params: Params | typeof idle, options?: QueryOptions): QueryResult

parameters.name: dataSource
parameters.description: Data source created with makePlainQueryDataSource or makeInfiniteQueryDataSource.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Query parameters, or idle to skip execution.

return_type: { data: TData | undefined; status: DataLoaderStatus; error: TError | null; refetch: Function }
examples: const { data, status, error, refetch } = useQueryData(userDataSource, { userId: 123 });
const { data } = useQueryData(userDataSource, userId ? { userId } : idle);

name: useQueryResponses
description: React hook that combines multiple query results into a single aggregated status, error, refetch, and refetchErrored. Useful for pages that depend on multiple data sources.
when_to_use: Combining loading/error state of multiple parallel queries into one DataLoader status
signature: useQueryResponses(responses: QueryResult[]): { status: DataLoaderStatus; error: unknown; refetch: Function; refetchErrored: Function }

parameters.name: responses
parameters.description: Array of query results from useQueryData.

return_type: { status: DataLoaderStatus; error: unknown; refetch: Function; refetchErrored: Function }
examples:
const combined = useQueryResponses([user, posts]);
<DataLoader status={combined.status} error={combined.error} errorAction={combined.refetchErrored} LoadingView={Spinner} ErrorView={Error}>{children}</DataLoader>

name: useRefetchAll
description: React hook that returns a stable callback that calls refetch() on every provided query state. Used to trigger a full refresh of all combined queries at once.
when_to_use: Creating a single 'Refresh All' handler for multiple data sources

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: Array of query result objects that have a refetch method.

name: useRefetchErrored
description: React hook that returns a stable callback that calls refetch() only for query states that have an error. Used as the errorAction for DataLoader when multiple queries are combined.
when_to_use: Creating a 'Retry Failed' handler that selectively refetches only errored queries
signature: useRefetchErrored<TError>(states: { error: TError | null; refetch: Function }[]): () => void

parameters.name: states
parameters.description: Array of query result objects with error and refetch fields.

name: withCancellation
description: Wraps a fetch function to add AbortSignal cancellation support, forwarding the react-query cancellation signal automatically.
when_to_use: Making fetch functions cancellable when the react-query query is cancelled or unmounted
avoid: When the underlying fetch already handles AbortSignal natively

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: Fetch function to wrap with cancellation.

name: withCatch
description: Wraps a fetch function with standardized error handling, transforming thrown errors using the provided error handler.
when_to_use: Adding consistent error normalization to a fetch function
signature: withCatch(fn: Function, errorHandler: (error: unknown) => unknown): Function

parameters.name: errorHandler
parameters.description: Transforms errors thrown by the fetch function.

name: withDataManager
description: Higher-order component that injects the DataManager from context as a dataManager prop into the wrapped component. Useful for class components or components that need DataManager without hooks.
when_to_use: Injecting DataManager into a class component or a component that cannot use hooks directly
avoid: In function components — use useDataManager hook instead

parameters.name: Component
parameters.description: Component to wrap; must accept a dataManager prop.
